info.title: Meridian Current Account Service API
info.description: BIAN-compliant current account operations including deposits, withdrawals, and lien management for fund reservations.

get.summary: ListFinancialBookingLogs lists booking logs with optional filtering.

parameters.name: pagination.pageSize
parameters.description: page_size is the number of items to return (default: 50, max: 1000).

parameters.name: pagination.pageToken
parameters.description: page_token is an opaque token for fetching the next page.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
status filters by transaction status.

 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING: TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING means the transaction is captured but not yet posted.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED means the transaction has been posted to the ledger.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED means the transaction posting failed.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED means the transaction was cancelled.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED means the transaction was reversed.

parameters.name: businessUnitReference
parameters.description: business_unit_reference filters by business unit.

post.summary: InitiateFinancialBookingLog creates a new financial booking log.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: InitiateFinancialBookingLogRequest creates a new financial booking log.

post.summary: CaptureLedgerPosting creates a new ledger posting.

parameters.name: financialBookingLogId
parameters.description: financial_booking_log_id references the parent booking log.

get.summary: RetrieveFinancialBookingLog retrieves a specific booking log.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the booking log to retrieve.

put.summary: UpdateFinancialBookingLog updates an existing booking log.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the booking log to update.

post.summary: InitiateCurrentAccount creates a new current account facility

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: account_id is the account to retrieve

post.summary: ExecuteDeposit processes a deposit transaction (Behavior Qualifier)

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: account_id is the account to deposit to

post.summary:
InitiateLien creates a fund reservation on an account
Used by Payment Order to reserve funds before external payment execution

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: account_id is the account to place the lien on

get.summary: Check performs a health check of the service.

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: service is the name of the service to check (optional).

parameters.name: lienId
parameters.description: lien_id is the lien to retrieve

post.summary:
ExecuteLien converts a reservation to an actual debit atomically
Called when the external payment is confirmed as settled

parameters.name: lienId
parameters.description: lien_id is the lien to execute

post.summary:
TerminateLien releases a reservation without executing
Called when the external payment fails or is cancelled

parameters.name: lienId
parameters.description: lien_id is the lien to terminate

get.summary:
ListPaymentOrders returns a paginated list of payment orders with optional filtering.
Supports filtering by debtor_account_id, status, and date range.

parameters.name: debtorAccountId
parameters.description: debtor_account_id filters by the internal account from which funds are debited.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
status filters by payment order status. Empty means all statuses.

 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_INITIATED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_INITIATED means the payment order has been created and validated.
Next: RESERVED (on lien success), FAILED (on validation error), CANCELLED (on user request)
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_RESERVED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_RESERVED means funds have been reserved via a lien on the debtor account.
Next: EXECUTING (on gateway send), FAILED (on error), CANCELLED (on user request)
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_EXECUTING: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_EXECUTING means the payment has been sent to the external gateway.
Next: COMPLETED (on gateway success), FAILED (on gateway rejection)
Note: Cannot be cancelled - must wait for gateway response
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_COMPLETED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_COMPLETED means the payment has been settled and funds transferred.
Terminal state. Can transition to REVERSED for post-completion compensation.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_FAILED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_FAILED means the payment failed at some stage of the saga.
Terminal state. Compensation (lien release) is triggered automatically.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_CANCELLED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_CANCELLED means the payment was cancelled before completion.
Terminal state. Different from FAILED: CANCELLED is intentional, FAILED is due to error.
Compensation (lien release) is triggered if status was RESERVED.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_REVERSED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_REVERSED means the payment was reversed after completion (compensation).
Terminal state. Used for post-completion corrections (e.g., refunds, chargebacks).

parameters.name: dateRange.startDate
parameters.description: start_date is the beginning of the period (inclusive) in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: dateRange.endDate
parameters.description: end_date is the end of the period (inclusive) in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: pagination.pageSize
parameters.description: page_size is the number of items to return (default: 50, max: 1000).

parameters.name: pagination.pageToken
parameters.description: page_token is an opaque token for fetching the next page.

post.summary:
InitiatePaymentOrder creates a new payment order and begins the saga.
The saga flow: INITIATED → RESERVED → EXECUTING → COMPLETED/FAILED

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: InitiatePaymentOrderRequest creates a new payment order (BQ: Initiate).

get.summary: RetrievePaymentOrder gets payment order details by ID.

parameters.name: paymentOrderId
parameters.description: payment_order_id is the unique identifier of the payment order to retrieve.

put.summary:
UpdatePaymentOrder handles asynchronous gateway callbacks.
Called when the external gateway reports the final payment status.

parameters.name: paymentOrderId
parameters.description:
payment_order_id is the preferred lookup method when available.
This avoids race conditions if webhook arrives before EXECUTING state is persisted.
Either payment_order_id or gateway_reference_id must be provided (enforced at service layer).

post.summary:
CancelPaymentOrder cancels a payment order before completion.
Only valid for INITIATED or RESERVED states. EXECUTING payments cannot be cancelled.
If status is RESERVED, compensation will release the lien on the debtor account.

parameters.name: paymentOrderId
parameters.description: payment_order_id is the unique identifier of the payment order to cancel.

post.summary:
ReversePaymentOrder reverses a completed payment order (post-completion compensation).
Only valid for COMPLETED state. Creates compensating ledger entries to reverse
the original transaction and transitions the order to REVERSED.
Idempotent: returns success if already reversed with the same idempotency key.

parameters.name: paymentOrderId
parameters.description: payment_order_id is the unique identifier of the payment order to reverse.

get.summary: ListFinancialPositionLogs lists financial position logs with filtering.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: account_id filters logs by account (optional).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
status filters logs by current status (optional).

 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING: TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING means the transaction is captured but not yet posted.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED means the transaction has been posted to the ledger.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED means the transaction posting failed.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED means the transaction was cancelled.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED means the transaction was reversed.

parameters.name: dateRange.startDate
parameters.description: start_date is the beginning of the period (inclusive) in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: dateRange.endDate
parameters.description: end_date is the end of the period (inclusive) in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: pagination.pageSize
parameters.description: page_size is the number of items to return (default: 50, max: 1000).

parameters.name: pagination.pageToken

get.summary: ListLedgerPostings lists ledger postings with optional filtering.

parameters.name: pagination.pageSize
parameters.description: page_size is the number of items to return (default: 50, max: 1000).

parameters.name: pagination.pageToken
parameters.description: page_token is an opaque token for fetching the next page.

parameters.name: financialBookingLogId
parameters.description: financial_booking_log_id filters by parent booking log.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: account_id filters by account identifier.

parameters.name: postingDirection
parameters.description:
posting_direction filters by debit or credit.

 - POSTING_DIRECTION_UNSPECIFIED: POSTING_DIRECTION_UNSPECIFIED means the direction is unknown.
 - POSTING_DIRECTION_DEBIT: POSTING_DIRECTION_DEBIT increases debit accounts, decreases credit accounts.
 - POSTING_DIRECTION_CREDIT: POSTING_DIRECTION_CREDIT increases credit accounts, decreases debit accounts.

parameters.name: valueDateFrom
parameters.description: value_date_from filters postings on or after this date.

parameters.name: valueDateTo
parameters.description: value_date_to filters postings on or before this date.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: currency filters by currency code (from posting_amount).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
status filters by transaction status.

 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING: TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING means the transaction is captured but not yet posted.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED means the transaction has been posted to the ledger.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED means the transaction posting failed.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED means the transaction was cancelled.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED means the transaction was reversed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the posting to retrieve.

put.summary: UpdateLedgerPosting updates an existing ledger posting.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the posting to update.

currentBalance.title: current_balance is the actual account balance (can be negative if overdrawn)

availableBalance.title: available_balance is current balance plus overdraft limit (if enabled)

lastUpdated.title: last_updated is when the balance was last calculated

AccountBalance.title:
AccountBalance represents the current balance state of an account
Task 5.2: Account balance tracking

AccountStatus.description:
- ACCOUNT_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: ACCOUNT_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown
 - ACCOUNT_STATUS_ACTIVE: ACCOUNT_STATUS_ACTIVE means the account is operational and can process transactions
 - ACCOUNT_STATUS_FROZEN: ACCOUNT_STATUS_FROZEN means the account is temporarily suspended
 - ACCOUNT_STATUS_CLOSED: ACCOUNT_STATUS_CLOSED means the account is permanently closed
AccountStatus.title: AccountStatus defines the lifecycle state of a current account facility

transactionId.description: transaction_id is the unique identifier for this transaction (alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

accountId.description: account_id is the account this transaction belongs to (alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

direction.title: direction is whether this is a debit or credit

status.title: status is the current transaction status

description.title: description is a human-readable transaction description

reference.description: reference is an external reference or payment reference (alphanumeric with hyphens, underscores, slashes).

timestamp.title: timestamp is when the transaction occurred

AccountTransaction.title:
AccountTransaction represents a debit or credit transaction on an account
Task 5.3: Create debit/credit transaction operations

AccountType.description:
AccountType defines the type of account in BIAN terms.

 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: ACCOUNT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED means the account type is unknown.
 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_DEBIT: ACCOUNT_TYPE_DEBIT is a debit account (asset).
 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_CREDIT: ACCOUNT_TYPE_CREDIT is a credit account (liability).
 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_VOSTRO: ACCOUNT_TYPE_VOSTRO is a correspondent bank account (our books, their money).
 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_NOSTRO: ACCOUNT_TYPE_NOSTRO is a correspondent bank account (their books, our money).
 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_CURRENT: ACCOUNT_TYPE_CURRENT is a current account (demand deposit).
 - ACCOUNT_TYPE_SAVINGS: ACCOUNT_TYPE_SAVINGS is a savings account.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

auditId.description: audit_id is the unique identifier for this audit entry.

timestamp.description: timestamp is when this audit event occurred.

userId.description: user_id is the identifier of the user who performed the action.

action.description: action describes what action was performed (e.g., "created", "updated", "approved").

details.description: details provides additional context about the action.

ipAddress.description: ip_address is the IP address from which the action was performed (IPv4 or IPv6).

systemContext.description: system_context provides additional system-level context (e.g., service name, version).

AuditTrailEntry.description: AuditTrailEntry captures audit information for compliance.

accountId.description: account_id is the account for which to create the log.

initialEntry.description: initial_entry is the first transaction entry (optional).

BatchInitiateRequest.description: BatchInitiateRequest represents a single log creation request in a batch.

success.description: success indicates whether this individual log was created successfully.

log.description: log is the created log (present only if success is true).

errorMessage.description: error_message provides details if success is false.

accountId.description: account_id is the account ID from the request for easy correlation.

BatchInitiateResult.description: BatchInitiateResult contains the result for a single log creation in a batch.

entries.description: entries are the transaction entries to import.

auditEntry.description: audit_entry is an audit trail entry for this bulk import (required for compliance).

version.description: version is used for optimistic concurrency control.

BulkImportTransactionsBody.description: BulkImportTransactionsRequest imports multiple transactions at once.

log.description: log is the updated financial position log after import (always present on success).

importedCount.description: imported_count is the number of transactions successfully imported.

failedCount.description: failed_count is the number of transactions that failed to import.

failureDetails.description: failure_details provides details about any failures.

BulkImportTransactionsResponse.description: BulkImportTransactionsResponse returns the result of the bulk import.

cancellationReason.description: cancellation_reason explains why the payment is being cancelled.

cancelledBy.description: cancelled_by identifies who is requesting the cancellation.

idempotencyKey.description: idempotency_key ensures exactly-once processing of cancellation.

CancelPaymentOrderBody.description:
CancelPaymentOrderRequest cancels a payment order before completion (BQ: Control).
Can only be called when status is INITIATED or RESERVED. EXECUTING payments
cannot be cancelled as they must wait for the gateway response.

paymentOrder.description: payment_order is the cancelled payment order with status CANCELLED.

CancelPaymentOrderResponse.description: CancelPaymentOrderResponse returns the cancelled payment order.

postingAmount.description: posting_amount is the amount to post (must be positive).

accountId.description: account_id identifies the target account (alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

CaptureLedgerPostingBody.description:
CaptureLedgerPostingRequest creates a new ledger posting.

Double-Entry Bookkeeping:
- Individual postings are created separately (not balanced pairs)
- Balance validation (debits = credits) occurs at service layer
- Booking log can only transition to POSTED status when balanced
- Consider using batch operations for balanced posting pairs

ledgerPosting.description: ledger_posting is the newly created posting.

status.description: status is the health status of the service.

CheckResponse.description: CheckResponse is the response for a health check.

Currency.description:
Currency defines ISO 4217 currency codes commonly used in banking.

 - CURRENCY_UNSPECIFIED: CURRENCY_UNSPECIFIED means the currency is unknown.
 - CURRENCY_GBP: CURRENCY_GBP is British Pound Sterling.
 - CURRENCY_USD: CURRENCY_USD is United States Dollar.
 - CURRENCY_EUR: CURRENCY_EUR is Euro.
 - CURRENCY_JPY: CURRENCY_JPY is Japanese Yen.
 - CURRENCY_CHF: CURRENCY_CHF is Swiss Franc.
 - CURRENCY_CAD: CURRENCY_CAD is Canadian Dollar.
 - CURRENCY_AUD: CURRENCY_AUD is Australian Dollar.

accountId.description: account_id is the unique identifier for this account facility (alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

accountIdentification.description:
account_identification is the IBAN or other standard account identifier.
IBAN format: 2 letter country code + 2 check digits + up to 30 alphanumeric characters.

accountStatus.title: account_status is the current lifecycle state of the account

baseCurrency.title: base_currency is the primary currency for this account

createdAt.title: created_at is when the account facility was created

updatedAt.title: updated_at is when the account facility was last modified

currentBalance.title:
current_balance tracks the current account balance
Task 5.2: Implement account balance tracking and overdraft limits

overdraftLimit.title: overdraft_limit configures the overdraft facility for this account

transactionHistory.title:
transaction_history tracks recent transactions for this account
Task 5.4: Add transaction history and account status management features

CurrentAccountFacility.title:
CurrentAccountFacility represents a BIAN-compliant current account facility
Task 5.1: Define CurrentAccountFacility message with account identification and status management

startDate.description: start_date is the beginning of the period (inclusive) in YYYY-MM-DD format.

endDate.description: end_date is the end of the period (inclusive) in YYYY-MM-DD format.

DateRange.description: DateRange represents a period between two dates.

ExecuteDepositBody.title: Request to execute a deposit (Behavior Qualifier)

transactionId.title: transaction_id is the created transaction ID

newBalance.title: new_balance is the updated account balance

availableBalance.title: available_balance is the updated available balance

ExecuteLienBody.title:
Request to execute a lien (convert reservation to actual debit)
BQ: Execute - Atomically converts lien to debit and removes reservation

lien.title: lien is the executed lien with EXECUTED status

newBalance.title: new_balance is the account balance after the debit

availableBalance.title: available_balance is the updated available balance

transactionId.title: transaction_id is the debit transaction created

id.description: id is the unique identifier for this financial booking log.

financialAccountType.description: financial_account_type defines the type of account being used.

productServiceReference.description: product_service_reference identifies the financial product or service.

businessUnitReference.description: business_unit_reference identifies the business unit responsible.

chartOfAccountsRules.description: chart_of_accounts_rules defines the accounting rules to apply.

baseCurrency.description: base_currency is the currency for this booking log.

status.description: status represents the current lifecycle state.

createdAt.description: created_at is when this booking log was created.

updatedAt.description: updated_at is when this booking log was last updated.

postings.description:
postings contains all ledger postings associated with this log.
NOTE: This array may grow large for booking logs with many transactions.
Consider fetching postings separately via RetrieveLedgerPosting for pagination.
Double-entry balance validation occurs at the service layer when transitioning to POSTED status.

FinancialBookingLog.description:
FinancialBookingLog represents the BIAN Financial Booking Log entity.
It maintains records of financial transactions and their processing status.

logId.description: log_id is the unique identifier for this financial position log.

accountId.description: account_id is the account this log pertains to.

transactionLogEntries.description: transaction_log_entries contains all transaction entries for this position.

transactionLineage.description: transaction_lineage captures the relationship between transactions.

auditTrail.description: audit_trail contains the complete audit history.

statusTracking.description: status_tracking contains the current and historical status information.

createdAt.description: created_at is when this log was created.

updatedAt.description: updated_at is when this log was last updated.

version.description: version is used for optimistic concurrency control.

FinancialPositionLog.description: FinancialPositionLog represents a comprehensive log of financial position for an account or transaction set.

GatewayStatus.description:
GatewayStatus represents the status returned by an external payment gateway.

 - GATEWAY_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: GATEWAY_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - GATEWAY_STATUS_SETTLED: GATEWAY_STATUS_SETTLED means the gateway has confirmed the payment was successful.
 - GATEWAY_STATUS_REJECTED: GATEWAY_STATUS_REJECTED means the gateway has rejected the payment.
 - GATEWAY_STATUS_PENDING: GATEWAY_STATUS_PENDING means the gateway is still processing the payment.

key.description: key is a unique identifier for this request (UUID v4 recommended, alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

ttlSeconds.description: ttl_seconds is how long the key should be valid (optional).

IdempotencyKey.description: IdempotencyKey ensures exactly-once processing of requests.

customerId.title: customer_id is the customer who owns this account

InitiateCurrentAccountRequest.title: Request to initiate a new current account

accountId.title: account_id is the newly created account ID

facility.title: facility is the complete account facility details

InitiateCurrentAccountResponse.title: Response for initiating a current account

financialAccountType.description: financial_account_type defines the type of account.

baseCurrency.description: base_currency is the currency for transactions.

idempotencyKey.description: idempotency_key ensures exactly-once processing for the entire batch.

batchId.description: batch_id is an optional client-provided batch identifier for tracking.

results.description: results contains the outcome for each log creation request (same order as input).

batchId.description: batch_id is the batch identifier (client-provided or server-generated).

totalCount.description: total_count is the total number of requests in the batch.

successCount.description: success_count is the number of logs successfully created.

failureCount.description: failure_count is the number of logs that failed to create.

InitiateFinancialPositionLogBatchRespons.description: InitiateFinancialPositionLogBatchResponse returns results for all log creations.

accountId.description: account_id is the account for which to create the log.

initialEntry.description: initial_entry is the first transaction entry (optional).

InitiateFinancialPositionLogRequest.description: InitiateFinancialPositionLogRequest initiates a new financial position log.

log.description: log is the created financial position log (always present on success).

amount.title: amount is the amount to reserve

payment_order_reference identifies the Payment Order creating this lien
Forward slash allowed for hierarchical Payment Order references

idempotencyKey.title: idempotency_key ensures exactly-once lien creation for retry scenarios

InitiateLienBody.title:
Request to initiate a lien (reserve funds) on an account
BQ: Initiate - Creates a new fund reservation

lien.title: lien is the created lien with ACTIVE status

availableBalance.title: available_balance is the updated available balance after reservation

debtorAccountId.description: debtor_account_id is the internal account to debit.

creditorReference.description: creditor_reference is the external account to credit (IBAN format).

amount.title:
amount is the payment amount.
NOTE: Amount must be positive (> 0). This is enforced at the service layer
since CEL validation on MoneyAmount wrapper types is not supported.
See README "Money Field Validation Limitations"

correlationId.description: correlation_id is optional for distributed tracing (generated if not provided).

InitiatePaymentOrderRequest.description: InitiatePaymentOrderRequest creates a new payment order (BQ: Initiate).

paymentOrder.description: payment_order is the newly created payment order.

InitiatePaymentOrderResponse.description: InitiatePaymentOrderResponse returns the created payment order.

id.description: id is the unique identifier for this posting.

financialBookingLogId.description: financial_booking_log_id references the parent booking log.

postingAmount.description: posting_amount is the monetary amount being posted (must be positive for accounting entries).

accountId.description: account_id identifies the account being posted to (alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

valueDate.description: value_date is the effective date for this posting.

postingResult.description: posting_result describes the outcome of the posting operation.

createdAt.description: created_at is when this posting was created.

status.description: status represents the current state of this posting.

LedgerPosting.description: LedgerPosting represents a single posting operation in double-entry bookkeeping.

lienId.title: lien_id is the unique identifier for this lien

accountId.title: account_id is the account this lien belongs to

amount.title: amount is the reserved amount (must be positive, validated at service layer)

status.title:
status is the current lien lifecycle state
State transitions:
  ACTIVE → EXECUTED (via ExecuteLien - idempotent)
  ACTIVE → TERMINATED (via TerminateLien)
  EXECUTED → no further transitions allowed
  TERMINATED → no further transitions allowed

paymentOrderReference.title: payment_order_reference is the Payment Order that created this lien

createdAt.title: created_at is when the lien was created

updatedAt.title: updated_at is when the lien was last modified

Lien.title:
Lien represents a fund reservation (hold) on an account
Used by Payment Order to reserve funds before executing external payments
Invariant: Available Balance = Current Balance - Sum(Active Liens)

LienExecutionStatus.description:
LienExecutionStatus tracks the status of lien execution for completed payment orders.
This is used to track async retry attempts when ExecuteLien fails after payment completion.

 - LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is not set (payment not yet completed).
 - LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_PENDING: LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_PENDING means ExecuteLien has not been attempted yet or is in progress.
 - LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_SUCCEEDED: LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_SUCCEEDED means ExecuteLien completed successfully.
 - LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_FAILED: LIEN_EXECUTION_STATUS_FAILED means ExecuteLien failed after all retry attempts.
Requires manual reconciliation.

LienStatus.description:
- LIEN_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: LIEN_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED is the default value and should not be used
 - LIEN_STATUS_ACTIVE: LIEN_STATUS_ACTIVE means the funds are currently reserved
 - LIEN_STATUS_EXECUTED: LIEN_STATUS_EXECUTED means the lien was converted to an actual debit
 - LIEN_STATUS_TERMINATED: LIEN_STATUS_TERMINATED means the lien was released without execution
LienStatus.title:
LienStatus defines the lifecycle state of a fund reservation (lien)
A lien is a hold placed on funds to reserve them for a pending payment

financialBookingLogs.description: financial_booking_logs is the list of booking logs.

ListFinancialBookingLogsResponse.description: ListFinancialBookingLogsResponse returns a page of booking logs.

logs.description: logs are the matching financial position logs.

ListFinancialPositionLogsResponse.description: ListFinancialPositionLogsResponse returns a list of logs.

ledgerPostings.description: ledger_postings is the list of postings.

ListLedgerPostingsResponse.description: ListLedgerPostingsResponse returns a page of ledger postings.

paymentOrders.description: payment_orders is the list of payment orders matching the filter criteria.

pagination.description: pagination contains the next page token and total count.

ListPaymentOrdersResponse.description: ListPaymentOrdersResponse returns a paginated list of payment orders.

currencyCode.description: The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

units.description:
The whole units of the amount.
For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

nanos.description:
Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

Money.description: Represents an amount of money with its currency type.

MoneyAmount.description:
MoneyAmount wraps google.type.Money with validation for financial services.
This allows negative amounts for balances and adjustments.
For strictly positive amounts (transactions, fees, limits), the value must be > 0
which is enforced at the service layer for business logic.

overdraftLimit.title: overdraft_limit is the maximum overdraft amount allowed

interestRate.title: interest_rate is the annual percentage rate for overdraft usage

isEnabled.title: is_enabled indicates if overdraft facility is active

lastUpdated.title: last_updated is when the overdraft configuration was last modified

OverdraftConfiguration.title:
OverdraftConfiguration defines the overdraft facility settings
Task 5.2: Overdraft limits

pageSize.description: page_size is the number of items to return (default: 50, max: 1000).

pageToken.description: page_token is an opaque token for fetching the next page.

Pagination.description: Pagination represents pagination parameters for list operations.

nextPageToken.description: next_page_token is the token for fetching the next page (empty if last page).

totalCount.description: total_count is the total number of items (if known, -1 otherwise).

PaginationResponse.description: PaginationResponse contains pagination metadata in responses.

paymentOrderId.description: payment_order_id is the unique identifier for this payment order.

debtorAccountId.description: debtor_account_id is the internal account ID from which funds are debited.

creditorReference.description:
creditor_reference is the external account identifier (IBAN or other standard).
IBAN format: 2 letter country code + 2 check digits + up to 30 alphanumeric characters.

amount.title:
amount is the payment amount.
NOTE: Amount must be positive (> 0). This is enforced at the service layer
since CEL validation on MoneyAmount wrapper types is not supported.
See README "Money Field Validation Limitations"

status.description: status is the current state in the payment saga lifecycle.

lienId.description: lien_id references the reservation created in CurrentAccount (populated after RESERVED).

gatewayReferenceId.description: gateway_reference_id is the external ID from the payment gateway (populated after EXECUTING).

correlationId.description: correlation_id is used for distributed tracing and request correlation.

idempotencyKey.description: idempotency_key ensures exactly-once processing of this payment order.

failureReason.description: failure_reason describes why the payment failed (populated only if status is FAILED).

createdAt.description: created_at is when this payment order was initiated.

updatedAt.description: updated_at is when this payment order was last modified.

ledgerBookingId.description: ledger_booking_id references the FinancialAccounting booking log (populated after COMPLETED).

reservedAt.description: reserved_at is when funds were reserved via lien.

executingAt.description: executing_at is when the payment was sent to the gateway.

completedAt.description: completed_at is when the payment was settled.

failedAt.description: failed_at is when the payment failed.

cancelledAt.description: cancelled_at is when the payment was cancelled.

reversedAt.description: reversed_at is when the payment was reversed (post-completion compensation).

causationId.description: causation_id identifies the event or command that caused the last state change.

errorCode.description: error_code provides a machine-readable error identifier (populated only if status is FAILED).

lienExecutionStatus.description:
lien_execution_status tracks whether the lien has been successfully executed.
Only relevant for COMPLETED payment orders with a lien_id.
Used for reconciliation when ExecuteLien fails after payment completion.

lienExecutionAttempts.description:
lien_execution_attempts tracks how many times ExecuteLien has been attempted.
Used for monitoring and alerting on retry exhaustion.

lienExecutionError.description:
lien_execution_error stores the last error message from ExecuteLien.
Only populated if lien_execution_status is FAILED.

PaymentOrder.description:
PaymentOrder represents the BIAN Payment Order control record.
It acts as the saga orchestrator for money movement, coordinating
across CurrentAccount (reservations) and FinancialAccounting (ledger booking).

PaymentOrderStatus.description:
PaymentOrderStatus defines the lifecycle state of a payment order in the saga.

State Machine:
  INITIATED → RESERVED → EXECUTING → COMPLETED (happy path)

Failure transitions (from any non-terminal state):
  INITIATED → FAILED (validation failure, account not found)
  RESERVED  → FAILED (gateway rejection, timeout) - triggers lien release
  EXECUTING → FAILED (gateway rejection) - triggers lien release

Cancellation (user/system initiated before completion):
  INITIATED → CANCELLED (no compensation needed)
  RESERVED  → CANCELLED (triggers lien release)
  EXECUTING → not cancellable (must wait for gateway response)

Reversal (post-completion compensation):
  COMPLETED → REVERSED (triggers compensating ledger entries)

Terminal states: COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED, REVERSED

 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_INITIATED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_INITIATED means the payment order has been created and validated.
Next: RESERVED (on lien success), FAILED (on validation error), CANCELLED (on user request)
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_RESERVED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_RESERVED means funds have been reserved via a lien on the debtor account.
Next: EXECUTING (on gateway send), FAILED (on error), CANCELLED (on user request)
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_EXECUTING: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_EXECUTING means the payment has been sent to the external gateway.
Next: COMPLETED (on gateway success), FAILED (on gateway rejection)
Note: Cannot be cancelled - must wait for gateway response
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_COMPLETED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_COMPLETED means the payment has been settled and funds transferred.
Terminal state. Can transition to REVERSED for post-completion compensation.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_FAILED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_FAILED means the payment failed at some stage of the saga.
Terminal state. Compensation (lien release) is triggered automatically.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_CANCELLED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_CANCELLED means the payment was cancelled before completion.
Terminal state. Different from FAILED: CANCELLED is intentional, FAILED is due to error.
Compensation (lien release) is triggered if status was RESERVED.
 - PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_REVERSED: PAYMENT_ORDER_STATUS_REVERSED means the payment was reversed after completion (compensation).
Terminal state. Used for post-completion corrections (e.g., refunds, chargebacks).

PostingDirection.description:
PostingDirection defines the direction of a financial posting.

 - POSTING_DIRECTION_UNSPECIFIED: POSTING_DIRECTION_UNSPECIFIED means the direction is unknown.
 - POSTING_DIRECTION_DEBIT: POSTING_DIRECTION_DEBIT increases debit accounts, decreases credit accounts.
 - POSTING_DIRECTION_CREDIT: POSTING_DIRECTION_CREDIT increases credit accounts, decreases debit accounts.

facility.title: facility is the current account facility

financialBookingLog.description: financial_booking_log is the retrieved booking log.

RetrieveFinancialBookingLogResponse.description: RetrieveFinancialBookingLogResponse returns the requested booking log.

log.description: log is the requested financial position log (always present on success).

paymentOrder.description: payment_order is the retrieved payment order.

RetrievePaymentOrderResponse.description: RetrievePaymentOrderResponse returns the requested payment order.

reversalReason.description: reversal_reason explains why the payment is being reversed.

reversedBy.description: reversed_by identifies who is requesting the reversal.

idempotencyKey.description: idempotency_key ensures exactly-once processing of reversal.

correlationId.description: correlation_id is optional for distributed tracing (generated if not provided).

ReversePaymentOrderBody.description:
ReversePaymentOrderRequest reverses a completed payment order (BQ: Exchange).
This creates compensating ledger entries and transitions the order to REVERSED.
Can only be called when the payment order is in COMPLETED state.

paymentOrder.description: payment_order is the reversed payment order with status REVERSED.

ReversePaymentOrderResponse.description: ReversePaymentOrderResponse returns the reversed payment order.

ServingStatus.description:
ServingStatus describes the health status of a service.

 - SERVING_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: SERVING_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - SERVING_STATUS_SERVING: SERVING_STATUS_SERVING means the service is healthy and serving requests.
 - SERVING_STATUS_NOT_SERVING: SERVING_STATUS_NOT_SERVING means the service is not serving requests.
 - SERVING_STATUS_UNKNOWN: SERVING_STATUS_UNKNOWN means the service health status cannot be determined.

currentStatus.description: current_status is the current status of the transaction.

previousStatus.description: previous_status is the previous status before the current one.

statusUpdatedAt.description: status_updated_at is when the status was last updated.

statusReason.description: status_reason provides context for status changes.

failureReason.description: failure_reason provides details if the transaction failed.

StatusTracking.description: StatusTracking captures the lifecycle status of the financial position log.

reason.title: reason is an optional explanation for termination (for audit trail)

TerminateLienBody.title:
Request to terminate a lien (release reserved funds)
BQ: Terminate - Releases the reservation without executing

lien.title: lien is the terminated lien with TERMINATED status

availableBalance.title: available_balance is the updated available balance after release

accountId.title: account_id is the account this history belongs to

transactions.title: transactions is the list of transactions (most recent first)

totalCount.title: total_count is the total number of transactions (for pagination)

lastUpdated.title: last_updated is when the history was last updated

TransactionHistory.title:
TransactionHistory represents the transaction history for an account
Task 5.4: Add transaction history and account status management features

transactionId.description: transaction_id is the current transaction identifier (UUID format).

parentTransactionId.description: parent_transaction_id is the identifier of the parent transaction (optional, UUID format when present).

childTransactionIds.description: child_transaction_ids are identifiers of any child transactions (UUID format).

relatedTransactionIds.description: related_transaction_ids are identifiers of related transactions (UUID format, e.g., reversals, adjustments).

transactionType.description: transaction_type describes the type of transaction (e.g., "payment", "reversal", "adjustment").

createdAt.description: created_at is when this lineage record was created.

TransactionLineage.description: TransactionLineage captures the relationship between transactions.

entryId.description: entry_id is the unique identifier for this log entry.

transactionId.description: transaction_id is the identifier of the related transaction (UUID format).

accountId.description: account_id is the account involved in this transaction (alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores).

amount.description: amount is the monetary amount of this transaction.

direction.description: direction indicates whether this is a debit or credit posting.

timestamp.description: timestamp is when this transaction occurred.

description.description: description provides additional context for this entry.

reference.description:
reference is an external reference number (e.g., invoice number, payment reference).
Alphanumeric with hyphens, underscores, slashes for payment references.

TransactionLogEntry.description: TransactionLogEntry represents a single entry in the financial position log.

status.description: status is the new status to set.

chartOfAccountsRules.description:
chart_of_accounts_rules can be updated if needed.
If empty, the existing rules are preserved (optional update).
Validation: Min length is enforced only if the field is provided (service layer).

UpdateFinancialBookingLogBody.description:
UpdateFinancialBookingLogRequest updates an existing booking log.

Update Semantics:
- Only status and chart_of_accounts_rules can be modified after creation
- Other fields (account_type, currency, business_unit) are immutable
- Empty chart_of_accounts_rules preserves existing value (use IGNORE_IF_UNPOPULATED)
- Status transitions are validated at service layer (e.g., POSTED is terminal)

financialBookingLog.description: financial_booking_log is the updated booking log.

UpdateFinancialBookingLogResponse.description: UpdateFinancialBookingLogResponse returns the updated booking log.

newEntry.description: new_entry is a new transaction entry to add (optional).

statusUpdate.description: status_update is a status change to apply (optional).

auditEntry.description: audit_entry is an audit trail entry to add.

version.description: version is used for optimistic concurrency control.

UpdateFinancialPositionLogBody.description: UpdateFinancialPositionLogRequest updates an existing financial position log.

log.description: log is the updated financial position log (always present on success).

status.description: status is the new status to set.

postingResult.description:
posting_result describes the outcome of the posting operation.
If empty, the existing result is preserved (optional update).

UpdateLedgerPostingBody.description:
UpdateLedgerPostingRequest updates an existing ledger posting.

Update Semantics:
- Only status and posting_result can be modified after creation
- Amount, direction, and account_id are immutable once created
- Status transitions follow domain rules (e.g., POSTED is terminal)
- Empty posting_result preserves existing value

gatewayReferenceId.description:
gateway_reference_id is the external ID from the payment gateway.
Used to look up the corresponding payment order when payment_order_id is not available.
Optional if payment_order_id is provided.

gatewayStatus.description: gateway_status is the status reported by the payment gateway.

gatewayMessage.description: gateway_message is optional details from the gateway (e.g., rejection reason).

idempotencyKey.description:
idempotency_key ensures exactly-once processing of webhook callbacks.
Webhooks can be delivered multiple times, so this is required.

UpdatePaymentOrderBody.description:
UpdatePaymentOrderRequest handles asynchronous gateway callbacks (BQ: Update).
This is typically called from the webhook HTTP adapter when the gateway
reports the final status of a payment.

Lookup Strategy:
The service uses payment_order_id if provided, otherwise falls back to
gateway_reference_id lookup. At least one must be provided.

Race Condition Handling:
If a webhook arrives before the EXECUTING state is persisted (race condition),
the service should:
1. If payment_order_id provided: Look up directly and process
2. If only gateway_reference_id: Return 404/retry-after, webhook will be redelivered
The idempotency_key ensures safe reprocessing when the webhook is retried.

paymentOrder.description: payment_order is the updated payment order.

UpdatePaymentOrderResponse.description: UpdatePaymentOrderResponse returns the updated payment order.

common.v1.TransactionStatus.description:
TransactionStatus defines the lifecycle state of a transaction.

 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED means the status is unknown.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING: TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING means the transaction is captured but not yet posted.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_POSTED means the transaction has been posted to the ledger.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED means the transaction posting failed.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_CANCELLED means the transaction was cancelled.
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED means the transaction was reversed.

current_account.v1.TransactionStatus.description:
- TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED is the default value and should not be used in requests
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING: TRANSACTION_STATUS_PENDING indicates the transaction is awaiting processing
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_COMPLETED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_COMPLETED indicates the transaction has been successfully processed
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_FAILED indicates the transaction processing failed
 - TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED: TRANSACTION_STATUS_REVERSED indicates the transaction has been reversed or cancelled
current_account.v1.TransactionStatus.title: Transaction status for deposit/withdrawal operations
